questions.question_text: What is the minimum E&O insurance coverage required per claim for Wyoming licensees?
distractors: $50,000 per claim
$250,000 per claim
$500,000 per claim
questions.explanation: Wyoming requires all active licensees to maintain errors and omissions insurance with a minimum of $100,000 per claim and $500,000 aggregate coverage under W.S. 33-28-401. This has been mandatory since January 1, 2008.

questions.question_text: A licensee renews her license but fails to submit proof of E&O insurance. What action can WREC take?
distractors: Issue a 90-day provisional renewal
Impose a late fee but approve renewal
Refer the matter to law enforcement
questions.explanation: Failure to maintain or provide proof of E&O insurance is grounds for license suspension in Wyoming. Licensees must submit proof when obtaining, renewing, or activating their license. WREC cannot allow active practice without verified insurance coverage.

questions.question_text: What is the maximum deductible allowed on a Wyoming licensee's E&O policy?
questions.explanation: Wyoming caps the E&O insurance deductible at $1,000 per claim. This low maximum ensures licensees can afford to activate their coverage when a claim arises, providing meaningful consumer protection rather than theoretical coverage with a prohibitive deductible.

questions.question_text: A broker activates a license that has been on inactive status for two years. When must E&O insurance proof be provided?
distractors: Within 60 days of the first transaction
At the next annual renewal date
Only if WREC requests verification
questions.explanation: Wyoming requires proof of E&O insurance when obtaining, renewing, or activating a license. Reactivating a dormant license triggers the same insurance verification requirement. The licensee cannot practice until valid coverage is confirmed.

questions.question_text: A salesperson allows her E&O policy to lapse mid-year but continues showing properties and writing offers. What is her regulatory exposure?
questions.correct_answer: License suspension for lack of E&O coverage
distractors: No risk until the next renewal cycle
A fine only if a claim is filed
Automatic transfer to inactive status
questions.explanation: All active Wyoming licensees must maintain continuous E&O insurance. A lapse at any point, not just at renewal, constitutes a violation. WREC can suspend the license, and any transactions conducted without coverage expose both the salesperson and the responsible broker to discipline.

questions.question_text: What entity contracts with an insurer to provide the Wyoming group E&O program?
distractors: The Wyoming Association of Realtors
The state Department of Insurance
Each individual brokerage
questions.explanation: WREC contracts directly with an insurance provider to offer a group E&O insurance program for licensees. This state-administered program ensures affordable, accessible coverage that meets the statutory minimum requirements.

questions.question_text: A licensee finds an individual E&O policy for $200 per year with $75,000 per claim coverage. Can she use this instead of the WREC group program?
questions.correct_answer: No, it does not meet the $100,000 minimum
distractors: Yes, any E&O policy satisfies the law
Yes, if the premium is lower than the group rate
No, individual policies are never permitted
questions.explanation: While licensees may obtain individual E&O coverage instead of the WREC group program, the policy must meet WREC's minimum requirements of $100,000 per claim and $500,000 aggregate. A $75,000 per claim policy falls short of the statutory minimum.

questions.question_text: A licensee prefers to get their own E&O policy rather than use the WREC group program. What must the individual policy meet?
distractors: Any coverage amount the licensee chooses
Double the group program coverage limits
Individual policies are not permitted
questions.explanation: Licensees may obtain individual E&O coverage instead of the WREC group program, but the policy must meet WREC's minimum requirements ($100,000 per claim / $500,000 aggregate with a deductible not exceeding $1,000). The group program is an option, not a mandate.

questions.question_text: A new licensee wants to opt out of the WREC group program entirely and go uninsured. Is this permitted?
questions.correct_answer: No, all active licensees must carry E&O
distractors: Yes, the group program is optional
Yes, for the first year of licensure
No, but only brokers must be insured
questions.explanation: E&O insurance is mandatory for all active Wyoming licensees, not just brokers. While participation in the WREC group program is optional (licensees can obtain qualifying individual coverage), carrying no E&O insurance at all is prohibited and grounds for suspension.

questions.question_text: A broker obtains an individual E&O policy with $100,000 per claim and $500,000 aggregate but a $2,500 deductible. Does this meet WREC standards?
distractors: Yes, coverage amounts are sufficient
Yes, deductibles are not regulated
No, aggregate must be $1,000,000
questions.explanation: While the per-claim and aggregate limits meet WREC minimums, the $2,500 deductible exceeds the statutory maximum of $1,000. All three requirements (per-claim minimum, aggregate minimum, and deductible cap) must be satisfied for the policy to qualify.
